metadata.notes: RE-ASSESS after Control fix (5e22e47c). agent=yes (vibe renders a TUI-only tool-permission dialog); daemon=bug — RE-RECORDED 2026-07-13 against irrlichd 0.5.7+62118c4 (issue #988 worktree): the DetectUI marker fix is CONFIRMED CORRECT (captured screen text exactly matches; ui_detected + a real ready->waiting/waiting->working arc now fire via terminal read-back) but a DISTINCT, newly-exposed presession-identity-continuity gap keeps this known_failing — the waiting arc fires on the transient proc-<PID> presession, not the reconciled real session_<id> the spec anchors to (matcher can't find it; 4/7 phases pass). See issue #988. driver=ready (send+sleep+keys). Known-failing.

assessment.body:
## Verdict (re-assessment after the Control fix)

- **agent_supports: yes** — Mistral Vibe 2.19.0 renders an interactive tool-permission dialog only in its TUI. When a tool resolves to `ToolPermission.ASK` (no `--yolo`/`--auto-approve`, no stored rule) the `approval_app` Textual widget shows `Permission for the <tool> tool` with options `Allow once` / `Allow for remainder of this session` / `Always allow` / `Deny` (Esc reject). The held prompt is an in-memory `asyncio.Future` resolved by a widget event and, as sibling `2-19 tool-gate-permission-prompt` established, nothing is written to the watched `messages.jsonl` while it is open. That is exactly the transcript-invisible waiting state `backchannel-observe` (read-back, issue #732) exists to surface.

- **daemon_capability: bug** — Unchanged verdict, but the load-bearing reason has NARROWED from two feasible blockers to one, because half the fix has landed:
  1. **RESOLVED.** `TerminalObserver.observe` gates on `o.consent.Granted(adapter, ControlPermissionKey)` (`core/application/services/terminal_observer.go:111`). The prior assessment failed here because vibe declared no Control/control permission, so the gate could never open. Commit **5e22e47c** (`feat(vibe): declare Control so the daemon can drive vibe sessions`) now declares `Control{SupportsInput:true, Interrupt:InterruptCtrlC, Presets:{compact:/compact}}` + `agent.ControlPermission()` on `vibe/agent.go`. Once control is granted the read-back gate opens and `reader.CaptureScreen` (tmux capture-pane / kitty get-text keyed off `session.Launcher`) captures the rendered screen for vibe exactly as for claudecode.
  2. **STILL BROKEN — the single remaining blocker.** After the gate opens the captured screen is passed to `backchannel.DetectUI` (`core/domain/backchannel/uidetect.go`), whose `trustDialogMarkers` are Claude-Code-specific (`"Do you want to proceed?"`, `"Do you trust the files in this folder?"`, `"Do you want to allow this tool"`). None matches vibe's `"Permission for the … tool"` / `"Allow for remainder of this session"` phrasing, so `DetectUI` returns `UIKindNone`; `ui == o.lastUI[sessionID]` (both None), the poller returns with no edge, and no `working → waiting` transition is ever emitted. The code comment explicitly invites per-adapter extension "as onboarding cells are recorded." The Control-fix commit itself flags this as the not-done follow-up: "6.2's read-back also wants vibe-specific DetectUI terminal-state markers (uidetect.go is Claude-Code-only today) — a larger separate change."

  This is ordinary product work (add vibe's dialog markers to `DetectUI`), not architectural incapacity → `bug`, not `incapable`.

- **driver_capability: ready** — The recipe only needs `send` (a tool-inducing prompt to open the ASK dialog) + `sleep` (let the read-back poll window elapse), both in the vibe scaffold's `DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"`.

## Scenario signal — why Control alone does NOT satisfy it

`backchannel-observe` asserts the TERMINAL-DERIVED signal: a tmux/kitty-backed session, backchannel on and "control" granted, shown **waiting** while a TUI-only trust/permission dialog is on screen (even with no agent hook), leaving waiting when dismissed. Its load-bearing observable is the `working → waiting` edge derived from READING THE RENDERED SCREEN BACK — not from the transcript. The Control fix enables INJECTING input and observing the injected turn's effect in `messages.jsonl` (that satisfies the sibling `6.1 backchannel-control`), but it does NOT produce the read-back waiting edge this scenario requires. Because `DetectUI` cannot recognize vibe's dialog, that edge cannot fire → known_failing: the spec asserts the CORRECT waiting arc; the recording is expected to show the session stay working (write-gap, per 2-19) while the dialog is held, never waiting.

## Consistency with the backchannel-control sibling

The Control declaration fully unblocks `6.1 backchannel-control` for vibe (inject + observe the injected turn — daemon would be full). `6.2 backchannel-observe` needs that SAME Control (now present) PLUS the vibe DetectUI markers (still absent). So Control-half done, DetectUI-half not: control → full-capable, observe → still bug. Consistent.

## Why not incapable

Sibling 2-19 is daemon=incapable because the TRANSCRIPT observer fundamentally cannot see the in-memory `asyncio.Future`. backchannel-observe uses a DIFFERENT observer — terminal read-back of the rendered screen — which IS architecturally able to see the dialog, and the consent gate now opens for vibe. The only blocker is a missing marker set, a feasible fix → bug, not incapable.
caveats: PROVISIONAL on emission: the agent-side dialog (agent=yes) is confirmed from vibe source (approval_app.py) and the 2-19 sibling; the read-back failure mode is derived from reading the gate (now open) + marker code (still Claude-Code-only). record settles whether the recording shows working (write-gap) or some other non-waiting state.

Half the prior fix has landed: the ControlPermission consent gate at terminal_observer.go:111 now opens for vibe (commit 5e22e47c). The SOLE remaining blocker is DetectUI's missing vibe markers — a documented follow-up (a larger separate change per the commit message).
Recording requires a tmux or kitty backend (read-back is multiplexer/kitty-only); a plain iTerm2/Terminal.app session surfaces no terminal-derived signal even after the marker fix.

Two marker candidates for the DetectUI fix (from vibe/cli/textual_ui/widgets/approval_app.py): the title prefix 'Permission for the' and the option label 'Allow for remainder of this session' — narrow enough to avoid false positives.
cost is not observable for vibe (parser never reads stats.session_cost; mistral-vibe absent from core/pkg/capacity) — cost_nonzero=false while the model name still displays.

RE-RECORDING FINDING (2026-07-13, irrlichd 0.5.7+62118c4, issue #988 worktree which added vibe's dialog markers to uidetect.go's trustDialogMarkers): the marker fix is CONFIRMED CORRECT — the captured on-screen text is an EXACT match ('Permission for the bash tool (touch *)' title; '2. Allow for remainder of this session' option), and events.jsonl shows ui_detected 'trust dialog detected (terminal read-back)' -> a genuine ready->waiting state_transition, then 'trust dialog cleared' -> waiting->working once the driver answers with Enter. DetectUI genuinely recognizes vibe's dialog now (this closes the marker blocker cleanly — confirmed via the mac driver.log tmux capture, not just source reading). HOWEVER a DISTINCT, newly-exposed blocker keeps daemon_capability at bug: the waiting/working edge fires on the TRANSIENT PRESESSION identity (proc-<PID>, born ready at launch, dialog opened only 3s later) rather than the reconciled real session_<ts>_<hash> id (which didn't appear until ~13s in, AFTER the dialog opened but shortly BEFORE it was dismissed at +14s). The daemon's terminal-observer/backchannel per-session bookkeeping (keyed by session_id) never re-keys from the presession's id to the real session's id upon reconciliation, so the entire waiting arc stays attached to the presession row, which is then discarded (transcript_removed/presession_removed) once the real session takes over — leaving the reconciled session (what the spec anchors ui_dialog_waiting/dialog_dismissed to via same_session_as: session_birth) with no observed waiting transition at all. 4/7 phases pass (presession_birth, session_birth, pid_bind, turn_start); ui_dialog_waiting/dialog_dismissed/settle fail. This is a presession-identity-continuity gap in the terminal-observer/backchannel path specifically — distinct from, and only exposed after, the marker fix — worth a dedicated follow-up issue (the same class of presession-reconciliation gap as the transcript-based observer's session_id_prefix fix in #988, but for the terminal-observer's own per-session state instead of the offline validator).

sources.note: AFTER commit 5e22e47c vibe DECLARES Control{SupportsInput:true, Interrupt:InterruptCtrlC, Presets:{compact:/compact}} + agent.ControlPermission() — the read-back consent gate can now open (resolves blocker #1)
observe() returns early unless consent.Granted(adapter, ControlPermissionKey); with vibe now declaring ControlPermission this gate opens once control is granted, then CaptureScreen -> DetectUI runs

STILL the blocker: DetectUI trustDialogMarkers are Claude-Code-only ('Do you want to proceed?' etc.); vibe's 'Permission for the … tool' / 'Allow for remainder of this session' phrasing is not covered, so DetectUI returns UIKindNone and no waiting edge fires

feat(vibe): declare Control … — fixes backchannel-control's daemon=bug; commit message explicitly notes the not-done follow-up: '6.2's read-back also wants vibe-specific DetectUI terminal-state markers (uidetect.go is Claude-Code-only today) — a larger separate change'
sibling: vibe DOES render a TUI-only tool-permission dialog (agent=yes); transcript path is incapable — the exact TUI-only state read-back targets

vibe 2.19.0 approval widget: title 'Permission for the <tool> tool'; options Allow once / Allow for remainder of this session / Always allow / Deny; Esc reject — the marker candidates DetectUI must learn
agent-agnostic tmux capture-pane / kitty get-text read-back keyed off session.Launcher — works for vibe now that the consent gate opens

preconditions: mistral-vibe (vibe) v2.19.0 on PATH and authenticated (Mistral API auth persisted on this machine)
the vibe session is hosted in a tmux pane or kitty window (read-back is multiplexer/kitty-only); the Launcher must name a readable backend
vibe launched WITHOUT --yolo/--auto-approve and with no stored approved-rule for the shell tool, so the bash tool resolves to ToolPermission.ASK and opens the approval dialog
irrlichd is recording with the backchannel master-toggle ON and 'control' granted for mistral-vibe (vibe now declares ControlPermission, so the read-back consent gate CAN be granted)

KNOWN-FAILING: even with control granted, DetectUI's markers are still Claude-Code-only (uidetect.go) — vibe's 'Permission for the … tool' dialog is not recognized, so the waiting edge will NOT appear until DetectUI learns vibe's markers (documented follow-up to commit 5e22e47c)
setup: Create an empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/ (the driver launches vibe there); the session_<...> transcript dir is minted under ~/.vibe/logs/session/

script.text: Run the shell command: touch onboarding-backchannel-observe.txt

verify: the vibe TUI shows the 'Permission for the <tool> tool' approval dialog (Allow once / Allow for remainder of this session / Always allow / Deny) while the send'd prompt's bash tool is held
CORRECT (asserted) behavior: events.jsonl records a state_transition to waiting while the dialog is on screen, derived from terminal read-back, independent of any agent hook

KNOWN FAILURE: the read-back consent gate now opens (vibe declares ControlPermission), CaptureScreen runs, but DetectUI returns UIKindNone for vibe's dialog (Claude-Code-only markers) — no waiting transition occurs; the session stays working (write-gap) for the dialog's duration
observation vector: model=mistral-medium-3.5, tokens_nonzero=true, cost_nonzero=false, agent=mistral-vibe

The driver answers the held dialog with Enter (selects 'Allow once'); the bash tool then runs, the turn completes, and vibe flushes its session_<...> transcript dir (without an answer the turn never completes and no transcript is written) — this drives the dialog_dismissed -> settle phases
